batch: Batch117 — Raw Next Build / Hosted Closure Triage
purpose: Keep raw Next build as the highest runtime truth while adding a deterministic diagnostic/report layer for Collecting page data hangs and hosted smoke readiness.
closureRequirements: npm run build:raw must exit 0 before raw build closure can be claimed
npm run build:raw:diagnose may record timeout/hang evidence but must not convert timeout into production-ready status
GIAOAN_SMOKE_MODE=production npm run live:smoke:clean must pass after a real .next build artifact exists
npm run auth-invite:runtime-smoke must pass before auth/session/CSRF runtime closure is claimed
GIAOAN_DEMO_URL=https://<hosted-url> npm run hosted:url-smoke must pass before hosted-ready is claimed

knownBlocker.meaning: In this audit environment, even a minimal Next app using Next 15.3.8 reached Collecting page data and did not exit within the bounded timeout. Treat this as unresolved until reproduced on target Node/Vercel host with full logs.
knownBlocker.nextReference: Next.js page-data/static-generation timeout guidance recommends checking unresolved promises, infinite loops, long network requests, and staticPageGenerationTimeout only as a diagnostic guard.

noOverclaimRules: Do not claim production-ready from guarded build artifacts
Do not claim hosted-ready without URL smoke
Do not claim raw build closure without build:raw exit 0
Do not add AI/payment/marketplace/verified academic data while runtime/build closure is unresolved
bmAdInspiredOperatingRules: Analyst: isolate the real blocker and evidence before feature work
PM: keep the batch to one capability and define acceptance gates
Architect: separate raw build truth from guarded fallback
Dev: make diagnostics repeatable instead of hiding failures
